content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of length MMM, we define the following:


The prefix sum array of BBB is an array PPP, also of length MMM, such that

Pi=B1+B2+…+BiP_i = B_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_iPi​=B1​+B2​+…+Bi​
BBB is called good, if every element of its prefix sum array is **not** a multiple of 333.
For example, B=[1,1,2,1]B = [1, 1, 2, 1]B=[1,1,2,1] is a good array, because its prefix sum array is [1,2,4,5][1, 2, 4, 5][1,2,4,5] and none of them are multiples of 333.

On the other hand, B=[1,1,2,2]B = [1, 1, 2, 2]B=[1,1,2,2] is not a good array, because its prefix sum array is [1,2,4,6][1, 2, 4, 6][1,2,4,6] and 666 is a multiple of 333.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Every element of AAA is either 111 or 222.

**At most once**, you can reverse a prefix of AAA.

Formally, this means you can choose an index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), and convert the array AAA into

[Ai,Ai−1,Ai−2,…,A2,A1,Ai+1,Ai+2,…,AN][A_i, A_{i-1}, A_{i-2}, \ldots, A_2, A_1, A_{i+1}, A_{i+2}, \ldots, A_N][Ai​,Ai−1​,Ai−2​,…,A2​,A1​,Ai+1​,Ai+2​,…,AN​]

Is it possible to make AAA good?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it's possible to make AAA good after a prefix reversal, and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e. the strings no,No,nO,\text{no}, \text{No}, \text{nO},no,No,nO, and NO\text{NO}NO will be treated as equivalent.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.output:
Yes
No
Yes
No
No
Yes
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The array is already good, since its prefix sum array is [1,2,4,5][1, 2, 4, 5][1,2,4,5].
Test case 222: [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has prefix sums [1,3][1, 3][1,3], and 333 is a multiple of 333.
The only prefix reversal that changes the array is to make it [2,1][2, 1][2,1], which has a prefix sum array of [2,3][2, 3][2,3].
In either case, AAA is not good, so the answer is "No".
Test case 333: Initially, A=[1,2,2,2,1]A = [1, 2, 2, 2, 1]A=[1,2,2,2,1], which is not good: its prefix sum array is [1,3,5,7,8][1, 3, 5, 7, 8][1,3,5,7,8].
We can reverse the prefix of length 333 to obtain [2,2,1,2,1][2, 2, 1, 2, 1][2,2,1,2,1], which is good: its prefix sum array is [2,4,5,7,8][2, 4, 5, 7, 8][2,4,5,7,8].
